title: trifolium cheranganiense
intro: Trifolium cheranganiense, also known as Cherangani clover, is a species of legume in the family Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Cherangani Hills in Kenya. The medicinal properties of Trifolium cheranganiense include its ability to inhibit inflammation and reduce oxidative stress, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, which are believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and improving digestion.
